text:
Linn (リン, Rin) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 209) is a player of the Greed Island game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 133 

Appearance

Linn has bug-like eyes and short hair, which peeks out from beneath his round hat. He also wears overalls with a short-sleeved shirt underneath and a pair of wrist-length gloves. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 183 

Plot

Greed Island arc

After Gon and Killua win their first specified slot card "Sword of Truth" in the city of Antokiba, Linn and several long-time players, including Kazsule and Asta, show up with the intention of stealing it. The players opt to decide the spell-casting order amongst them through rock-paper-scissors, though Kazsule ultimately obtains the card. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 151 

Eventually, he is shown to be still alive in the game as a bright dot can be seen next to his name in Gon's binder, indicating his status. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Following Gon's acquisition of 99 specified slot cards and the announcement of a final quiz, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 182 Linn teleports to Gon's group alongside Erbier Mano. The duo approach the group and offer to sell them "Ruler's Blessing" for 5% of Battera's reward (2.5 billion) if they win the quiz, unaware Battera cancelled the contract following his lover's death. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 174 Killua accepts, if they can manage to pull it off. The quiz begins, and Linn takes it with Erbier, though they ultimately lose to Gon. Despite their loss, Linn congratulates Gon on his victory. 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Linn is capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Linn's original Japanese name, リン (Rin), is identical to the original first name of Lin Koshi.
Though Linn's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook.
